마가복음 6:1.content: He went out from there. He came into his own country, and his disciples followed him.

마가복음 6:2.content: When the Sabbath had come, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many hearing him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things?” and, “What is the wisdom that is given to this man, that such mighty works come about by his hands?

마가복음 6:3.content: Isn’t this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James, Joses, Judah, and Simon? Aren’t his sisters here with us?” They were offended at him.

마가복음 6:4.content: Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country, and among his own relatives, and in his own house.”

마가복음 6:5.content: He could do no mighty work there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people, and healed them.

마가복음 6:6.content: He marveled because of their unbelief. He went around the villages teaching.

마가복음 6:7.content: He called to himself the twelve, and began to send them out two by two; and he gave them authority over the unclean spirits.

마가복음 6:8.content: He commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, except a staff only: no bread, no wallet, no money in their purse,

마가복음 6:9.content: but to wear sandals, and not put on two tunics.

마가복음 6:10.content: He said to them, “Wherever you enter into a house, stay there until you depart from there.

마가복음 6:11.content: Whoever will not receive you nor hear you, as you depart from there, shake off the dust that is under your feet for a testimony against them. Assuredly, I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city!”

마가복음 6:12.content: They went out and preached that people should repent.

마가복음 6:13.content: They cast out many demons, and anointed many with oil who were sick, and healed them.

마가복음 6:14.content: King Herod heard this, for his name had become known, and he said, “John the Baptizer has risen from the dead, and therefore these powers are at work in him.”

마가복음 6:15.content: But others said, “He is Elijah.” Others said, “He is a prophet, or like one of the prophets.”

마가복음 6:16.content: But Herod, when he heard this, said, “This is John, whom I beheaded. He has risen from the dead.”

마가복음 6:17.content: For Herod himself had sent out and arrested John, and bound him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, for he had married her.

마가복음 6:18.content: For John said to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”

마가복음 6:19.content: Herodias set herself against him, and desired to kill him, but she couldn’t,

마가복음 6:20.content: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and kept him safe. When he heard him, he did many things, and he heard him gladly.

마가복음 6:21.content: Then a convenient day came, that Herod on his birthday made a supper for his nobles, the high officers, and the chief men of Galilee.

마가복음 6:22.content: When the daughter of Herodias herself came in and danced, she pleased Herod and those sitting with him. The king said to the young lady, “Ask me whatever you want, and I will give it to you.”

마가복음 6:23.content: He swore to her, “Whatever you shall ask of me, I will give you, up to half of my kingdom.”

마가복음 6:24.content: She went out, and said to her mother, “What shall I ask?” She said, “The head of John the Baptizer.”

마가복음 6:25.content: She came in immediately with haste to the king, and asked, “I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptizer on a platter.”

마가복음 6:26.content: The king was exceedingly sorry, but for the sake of his oaths, and of his dinner guests, he didn’t wish to refuse her.

마가복음 6:27.content: Immediately the king sent out a soldier of his guard, and commanded to bring John’s head, and he went and beheaded him in the prison,

마가복음 6:28.content: and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the young lady; and the young lady gave it to her mother.

마가복음 6:29.content: When his disciples heard this, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.

마가복음 6:30.content: The apostles gathered themselves together to Jesus, and they told him all things, whatever they had done, and whatever they had taught.

마가복음 6:31.content: He said to them, “You come apart into a deserted place, and rest awhile.” For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.

마가복음 6:32.content: They went away in the boat to a deserted place by themselves.

마가복음 6:33.content: They saw them going, and many recognized him and ran there on foot from all the cities. They arrived before them and came together to him.

마가복음 6:34.content: Jesus came out, saw a great multitude, and he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.

마가복음 6:35.content: When it was late in the day, his disciples came to him, and said, “This place is deserted, and it is late in the day.

마가복음 6:36.content: Send them away, that they may go into the surrounding country and villages, and buy themselves bread, for they have nothing to eat.”

마가복음 6:37.content: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They asked him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread, and give them something to eat?”

마가복음 6:38.content: He said to them, “How many loaves do you have? Go see.” When they knew, they said, “Five, and two fish.”

마가복음 6:39.content: He commanded them that everyone should sit down in groups on the green grass.

마가복음 6:40.content: They sat down in ranks, by hundreds and by fifties.

마가복음 6:41.content: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed and broke the loaves, and he gave to his disciples to set before them, and he divided the two fish among them all.

마가복음 6:42.content: They all ate, and were filled.

마가복음 6:43.content: They took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and also of the fish.

마가복음 6:44.content: Those who ate the loaves were five thousand men.

마가복음 6:45.content: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat, and to go ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he himself sent the multitude away.

마가복음 6:46.content: After he had taken leave of them, he went up the mountain to pray.

마가복음 6:47.content: When evening had come, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and he was alone on the land.

마가복음 6:48.content: Seeing them distressed in rowing, for the wind was contrary to them, about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the sea, and he would have passed by them,

마가복음 6:49.content: but they, when they saw him walking on the sea, supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;

마가복음 6:50.content: for they all saw him, and were troubled. But he immediately spoke with them, and said to them, “Cheer up! It is I! Don’t be afraid.”

마가복음 6:51.content: He got into the boat with them; and the wind ceased, and they were very amazed among themselves, and marveled;

마가복음 6:52.content: for they hadn’t understood about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.

마가복음 6:53.content: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.

마가복음 6:54.content: When they had come out of the boat, immediately the people recognized him,

마가복음 6:55.content: and ran around that whole region, and began to bring those who were sick, on their mats, to where they heard he was.

마가복음 6:56.content: Wherever he entered, into villages, or into cities, or into the country, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might just touch the fringe of his garment; and as many as touched him were made well.
